Welcome to the Polyglot Guide | Meta Mode Guide

Welcome to Meta Mode — the centralized documentation for the framework and build-system features.

This mode is dedicated to explaining the inner workings of the Polyglot Dev Atlas. It serves as both a manual and a live testing environment for new engine features.

This entire guide is generated from JSON files located in content/pages/meta/. This page uses meta.css for styling with "style": "meta-style".

Getting Started

^

This guide explains how to use Meta Mode and the available utilities.

Usage

^

Use the mode switcher to enter Meta mode. Pages in this mode live under content/pages/meta/.

The Build Pipeline

^

The engine uses a 4-stage process to transform your data into a high-performance static site:

  1. Registry Scan: Python maps all JSON files to build the navigation sidebars.
  2. Schema Parsing: The recursive renderer translates JSON objects into HTML tags.
  3. Asset Syncing: CSS, JS, and Syntax Highlighting files are mirrored to the distribution folder.
  4. Persistence Injection: LocalStorage logic is baked into the headers to prevent UI flashing.

Core Technologies

^

The Atlas is designed to be zero-dependency on the client side, utilizing standard modern web APIs:

  • Python 3: The static site generator core.
  • CSS Grid & Variables: Powering the geometric design system and color palettes.
  • Intersection Observer: Driving the high-performance Scroll-Spy sidebar.
  • Highlight.js: Handling multi-language syntax highlighting.
▶
